text: Should the strict detector's operating point be chosen by the historical-recovery plateau (recall-calibrated) rather than the bridge-count elbow? Across theta>=2 strict recovery is flat at 2/8 while the count roughly doubles by theta=3 -- is there any theta (e.g. 3-5) that recovers a 3rd celebrated bridge without exploding false positives, or does recall genuinely cap at 2/8 for the premise-based method regardless of theta?
